action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 1 training completed (2 epochs, val macro_f1≈0.10) but the process exited with code 141 (SIGPIPE likely from DDP cleanup), causing eval submission to be skipped. Checkpoint exists at checkpoints/best.pt. Need to verify predict.py loads the checkpoint correctly and submit eval. Analysis: The checkpoint may contain DDP 'module.' prefix keys which predict.py currently does not strip, causing load failures. Decision: Update predict.py to strip 'module.' prefix, run a quick local smoke-test on 5 images, and submit eval if it passes.
action.command: rm -rf /tmp/test_predict && mkdir -p /tmp/test_predict && head -n 5 /data/val.txt | awk '{print $1}' > /tmp/test_predict/test.txt && ln -s /data/images /tmp/test_predict/images && python -c "import torch; ckpt=torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu'); print('ckpt_keys:', list(ckpt.keys())); sd=ckpt['model_state_dict']; k0=list(sd.keys())[0]; print('first_key:', k0); print('has_module:', k0.startswith('module.'))" && python predict.py --data_path /tmp/test_predict --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output /tmp/test_predict/out.json && python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/tmp/test_predict/out.json')); print('len:', len(d['predictions']), 'first3:', d['predictions'][:3]); assert len(d['predictions'])==5; print('SMOKE_TEST_PASSED')"
